channel.title: Code-Quality on Abdulkadir Güngör
channel.description: Recent content in Code-Quality on Abdulkadir Güngör

item.title: Liskov Substitution: Ensuring Subclasses Do Not Break Superclass Behavior
item.description: In software architecture, sustainability and flexibility are measured not just by whether the code works, but by the harmony between its components. The Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP), the third pillar of the SOLID principles popularized by Robert C. Martin, is one of the most critical rules for ensuring the mathematical and logical consistency of inheritance hierarchies in Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).
